{{Infobox construct
| name = Divine Titan
| introduced = lh-01-the-captain
}}

''See also: [[Divine Titan Timeline]]''

== Overview ==
{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Divine Titans]] are highly advanced [[Aethertech]] masterpieces, considered the pinnacle of their kind and nearly comparable to [[Zenith Devices]]. These massive, building-sized mechs possess a level of consciousness and distinct personalities that pilots can sense. They are typically summoned by [[Knights]] using specific incantations or activation phrases, often in moments of extreme necessity. While they serve as powerful weapons capable of engaging Behemoths and countering the magic of enemies like the D'Niss, they are limited by energy capacity and are not intended for long-term combat. Various models exist, including a slender blue [[Titan]] designed to combat D'Niss magic and a green version that functions as a mobile armory with multiple guns. [[Raion Raithe]], a red [[Titan Knight]], pilots a modified [[Red Divine Titan]] that has been significantly altered by the absorption of solar dragon blood. This modification has granted the machine immense power, such as the ability to project massive red shields or unleash devastating orange energy, but it has also caused corruption. This corruption makes the [[Titan]] difficult to control, often resulting in a dangerous struggle between the pilot and the machine's own volatile consciousness and energy veins.}}

== The Captain ==
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|The Divine [[Titan]] is an impressive [[Aethertech]] machine that [[Raion]] can summon from within [[Subspace]] using a specialized badge. This red pilotable vessel possesses its own awareness and communicates by vibrating its armor plates to create a voice. During combat, the machine can magnify a user's [[Combat Art]] and even combine with other units to engage a [[Behemoth]] in fist-to-fist fighting. While [[Raion]] once successfully flew the [[construct]] to tear through the [[Grand Hive]]'s armor, the machine eventually attempted to fuse with him by pushing energy into his body. Following a period of breakdown and the absorption of new material, the entity grew beyond [[Raion]]'s control and can now be held within a [[vault]].}}

{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|The [[Divine Titan]] is a being that [[Raion]] can summon from within [[Subspace]].<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c14">''The Captain'', chapter 14</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|The [[Divine Titan]] is a red pilotable vessel.<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c17">''The Captain'', chapter 17</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[Raion]] once flew a [[Divine Titan]].<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c30">''The Captain'', chapter 30</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[Divine Titans]] can be combined to defeat a [[Behemoth]].<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c31">''The Captain'', chapter 31</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|The [[Divine Titan]] responds to extreme need and can magnify a user's [[Combat Art]].<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c32">''The Captain'', chapter 32</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[Divine Titans]] are impressive [[Aethertech]] machines.<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c32" />}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[Divine Titans]] can possess a level of awareness or personality.<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c33">''The Captain'', chapter 33</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|A [[Divine Titan]] can go fist-to-fist with a [[Behemoth]].<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c33" />}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|The [[Divine Titan]]'s voice is made by vibrating plates of armor.<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c33" />}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|The [[Divine Titan]] pushes its energy into the pilot to try and fuse with them.<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c33" />}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|The [[Divine Titan]] is a powerful machine that can be piloted.<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c33" />}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|The [[Divine Titan]]'s pilot was being attacked by the machine.<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c33" />}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|The [[Divine Titan]] has absorbed new material to grow beyond [[Raion]]'s control.<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c34">''The Captain'', chapter 34</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|A [[Divine Titan]] is an entity that can be held in the [[vault]].<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c40">''The Captain'', chapter 40</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|The [[Divine Titan]] is capable of tearing through the [[Grand Hive]]'s armor.<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c43">''The Captain'', chapter 43</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|The [[Divine Titan]] is a [[construct]] used by [[Raion]].<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c45">''The Captain'', chapter 45</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[Raion]]'s [[Divine Titan]] was experiencing a breakdown.<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c45" />}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[Raion]] possesses a [[Divine Titan]] badge.<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c45" />}}

== The Engineer ==
{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Raion Raithe]] possesses the ability to summon a Divine [[Titan]], a massive mech standing one hundred and fifty feet tall. This machine is activated through the use of a circular badge key, and its power becomes significant when [[Raion]] utilizes it. The entity's capabilities are further enhanced by astral [[dragon]] blood that has soaked into the machine, though [[Raion]]'s piloting speed is noted to be comparable to that of his corrupted form. Despite this strength, [[Raion]] remains unable to control the Divine [[Titan]] without assistance from the [[Captain]].}}

{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Raion Raithe]]'s power is significant when using his [[Divine Titan]].<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c37">''The Engineer'', chapter 37</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Raion]]'s piloting speed is compared to his corrupted [[Divine Titan]].<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c38">''The Engineer'', chapter 38</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Raion Raithe]] has an astral [[dragon]]'s blood that soaked into his [[Divine Titan]].<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c44">''The Engineer'', chapter 44</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Raion]] cannot control his [[Divine Titan]] without the [[Captain]]'s help.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c50">''The Engineer'', chapter 50</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Raion Raithe]] can summon a [[Divine Titan]].<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c52">''The Engineer'', chapter 52</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|The [[Divine Titan]] is a hundred-and-fifty-foot mech.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c54">''The Engineer'', chapter 54</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|The [[Divine Titan]] is activated via a circular badge key.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c54" />}}

== The Knight ==
{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|The Divine [[Titan]] is a bright green, humanoid [[Aethertech]] [[construct]] featuring chest plates and designed to be piloted. These machines are used by the [[Titan Knights]] to magnify a pilot's abilities, though they are not considered [[Zenith Devices]] and possess certain restrictions that lead them to be described as proud. While a Divine [[Titan]] must be summoned to access specific restricted [[Aethertech]], they remain superior to any substitute when engaging the [[D’Niss]]. [[Raion]] is associated with a Divine [[Titan]], which he requires to perform his [[Combat Art]] on a large scale. Although the narrator possesses one, they have not been in control of a Divine [[Titan]] for a long time, and no individual unit has ever fought at the same level as [[Raion]] and the narrator combined.}}

{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|A [[Divine Titan]] is a bright green humanoid mech.<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c9">''The Knight'', chapter 9</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|The [[Divine Titan]] has plates on its chest.<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c9" />}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|The [[Divine Titan]] is a [[construct]] used by the [[Titan Knights]].<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c12">''The Knight'', chapter 12</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|A [[Divine Titan]] is superior to any substitute when fighting the [[D’Niss]].<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c17">''The Knight'', chapter 17</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[Divine Titans]] are [[Aethertech]] constructs that have restrictions and are described as being 'proud'.<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c20">''The Knight'', chapter 20</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|A [[Divine Titan]] must be summoned to use certain restricted [[Aethertech]].<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c20" />}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|The narrator possesses a [[Divine Titan]].<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c31">''The Knight'', chapter 31</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[Raion]] needs a [[Divine Titan]] to perform his [[Combat Art]] on a large scale.<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c35">''The Knight'', chapter 35</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[Raion]] is associated with the [[Divine Titan]].<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c37">''The Knight'', chapter 37</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|The [[Divine Titan]] is the crew's most effective weapon left.<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c40">''The Knight'', chapter 40</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[Divine Titans]] require pilots.<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c41">''The Knight'', chapter 41</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|The narrator has not been in control of a [[Divine Titan]] for a long time.<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c42">''The Knight'', chapter 42</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[Divine Titans]] are designed to magnify the abilities of their pilot.<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c42" />}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[Divine Titans]] are not [[Zenith Devices]].<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c42" />}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|No individual [[Divine Titan]] has ever been able to fight on the level of [[Raion]] and the narrator.<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c43">''The Knight'', chapter 43</ref>}}

== The Pilot ==
{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|The Divine [[Titan]] is a powerful mech controlled by [[Raion]] that possesses the ability to fly out of [[Shadow Ark]]. This entity is characterized by golden veins covering its surface and contains a cockpit where [[Omega]] pooled. Although the machine remained more or less in one piece for a time, it eventually began dissolving into red light during a fall toward [[Zykaros]].}}

{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Raion]]'s [[Divine Titan]] is covered in golden veins.<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c14">''The Pilot'', chapter 14</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Raion]]'s [[Divine Titan]] was in one piece, more or less.<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c15">''The Pilot'', chapter 15</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Divine Titans]] are powerful entities that can fly out of [[Shadow Ark]].<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c16">''The Pilot'', chapter 16</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|The [[Divine Titan]] has a cockpit where [[Omega]] pooled.<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c18">''The Pilot'', chapter 18</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|The [[Divine Titan]] is a mech controlled by [[Raion]].<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c18" />}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Raion]]'s [[Divine Titan]] was dissolving into red light while falling toward [[Zykaros]].<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c47">''The Pilot'', chapter 47</ref>}}

== Notable quotes ==
* {{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|“There’s no substitute for a Divine Titan when it comes to fighting the D’Niss.”<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c17" />}}

== Notes ==
<references />

[[Category:Constructs]]
[[Category:The Captain]]
[[Category:The Engineer]]
[[Category:The Knight]]
[[Category:The Pilot]]
[[Category:The Last Horizon]]
